nodes.norm_label: contributing guide (tdd, commit convention, code standards)

nodes.label: Graph (DAG model)
nodes.rationale: The graph is a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph). Cycles must be rejected. Edges can only connect ports of compatible types. UUIDs are used for IDs (never mutable integer indexes).

nodes.label: Graph Serialization (JSON)
nodes.rationale: UI state (selection) must NOT be stored in serialization. Position is OK to store. A schema_version field is required for future migrations.

nodes.label: DFS Colored Cycle Detection
nodes.rationale: 3-color DFS (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK). Reaching a GRAY node means a cycle was detected. Alternative: Kahn's algorithm (topological sort via BFS using in-degree tracking).

nodes.label: GraphScene (QGraphicsScene)
nodes.rationale: Scene does NOT modify the Graph model directly — it emits signals. Signal → Controller → Graph (MVC or Command pattern). For thousands of nodes use QGraphicsItem.setFlag(ItemUsesExtendedStyleOption) and Qt native culling.

nodes.label: EdgeItem (Bezier curve)
nodes.rationale: Bezier curve: P0=source port, P3=dest port, P1=P0+(100,0), P2=P3-(100,0). Uses QPainterPath.cubicTo.

nodes.label: NodeRegistry
nodes.rationale: Singleton (or global dict) mapping type → NodeDefinition. Allows adding custom nodes later via plugins.

nodes.label: Command Pattern (Undo/Redo)
nodes.rationale: Command ABC with execute()/undo(). CommandHistory with stack+index. MacroCommand for multi-step operations (e.g., paste = N AddNode + M AddEdge). History limit of ~100 commands to prevent memory leaks.

nodes.label: ComponentDefinition (Subgraph)
nodes.rationale: Components are nestable. Stored in ComponentLibrary. Must be recursively resolved at PyTorch code generation time.

nodes.label: Shape Inference (Tensor Propagation)
nodes.rationale: Uses topological sort (Kahn) to propagate shapes. Dimensions can be partially known (batch size = None). Uses Optional[int] for unknown dims. Propagation stops and raises error on incompatible shapes.

nodes.label: PyTorch Code Generator (Graph → nn.Module)
nodes.rationale: String-based code generation (not AST) for human readability. Output is debuggable by the user. Generation must be deterministic: same graph → same code. ComponentNodes resolved recursively into separate nn.Module subclasses.

nodes.label: TrainingConfig
nodes.rationale: Configs must be serializable for versioning in phase 3. Validate coherence: e.g., BCE loss + non-sigmoid output → warning.

nodes.label: TrainingWorker (subprocess)
nodes.rationale: Runs in a separate subprocess to keep UI reactive. UI reads queue via QTimer every 100ms (non-blocking). Checkpoints saved by worker via torch.save. Monitor worker process via .is_alive() to handle crashes.

nodes.label: Run (Experiment Tracking)
nodes.rationale: Stored in SQLite (no ORM, stdlib sqlite3). Metrics kept in memory during run, flushed to DB in batches. Index on experiment_name and start_time for comparison queries.

nodes.label: ObjectStore C++ (Content-Addressable)
nodes.rationale: Inspired by Git object model. SHA256(content) as path key. Atomic writes via temp file + rename (prevents corruption). Thread-safe: immutable files, concurrent reads OK. Deduplication is free. Files marked read-only (chmod 444). SHA256 via OpenSSL or picosha2 (zero-dep).

nodes.label: Snapshot
nodes.rationale: Snapshot chains form history via parent_hash (like git log). Contains architecture, hyperparameter, dataset, checkpoint, metrics hashes and a user message.

nodes.label: Forking (Resume from Checkpoint)
nodes.rationale: Checkpoint must include optimizer state, not only model weights. torch.save({'model': ..., 'optimizer': ..., 'epoch': ...}). Must validate that fork architecture is compatible with checkpoint weight shapes.

nodes.label: HookManager (PyTorch Hooks)
nodes.rationale: Hooks run in training process. Only scalar statistics sent to queue (not full tensors). Subsampling every N steps to avoid slowing training. Handles must be properly removed via handle.remove() at end of training.

nodes.label: RingBuffer C++ (Lock-Free SPSC)
nodes.rationale: Python multiprocessing.Queue has high latency and serialization overhead. A lock-free ring buffer in shared memory is much faster for scalar streaming. SPSC (Single Producer Single Consumer): trivially lock-free. Uses std::atomic with memory_order_acquire/release.

nodes.label: Live Graph Qt Update
nodes.rationale: QTimer at 100ms reads ring buffer. Only update nodes with changed metrics. QGraphicsItem.update() is async — Qt batches repaints. Avoid allocations in update slot (reuse QColor objects).

nodes.label: NodeInspector (QDockWidget)
nodes.rationale: Welford's online algorithm for mean/variance in a single pass. Histogram via torch.histc(tensor, bins=50) on training side, send bin counts. pyqtgraph recommended for rendering (lightweight, native Qt, real-time capable).

nodes.label: Visualization Panels
nodes.rationale: Activation maps for Conv2d only: send first image of batch, first channels. Offer snapshot mode (on demand) vs live mode (every N steps).

nodes.label: RemoteHost / SSHAuthConfig
nodes.rationale: Passwords/passphrases stored in system keyring (keyring library). Configurable connection timeout (default 10s). Auto-reconnect on session expiry.

nodes.label: Remote File Synchronization
nodes.rationale: Uses rsync via subprocess if available, falls back to paramiko SFTP. Smart sync: compute SHA256 of local files, only transfer missing or changed files. Remote venv created if needed.

nodes.label: RemoteTrainingController
nodes.rationale: Metrics streamed from remote via SSH stdout as JSON lines. Local thread reads SSH channel line-by-line, parses JSON, updates UI.

nodes.label: Remote Checkpoint Transfer
nodes.rationale: Checkpoints saved remotely, automatically repatriated every N epochs (configurable). Stored in local object store. Optional gzip compression before transfer. Advanced: sparse diff of weights for large models.

nodes.label: Advanced Profiling (PyTorch Profiler + pynvml)
nodes.rationale: Profiling slows training: offer a separate profiling mode. Profiling traces can be very large: limit capture duration. On remote machine: run profiler, repatriate trace, visualize locally.

nodes.label: NeuroForge Vision & Core Principles
nodes.rationale: Desktop-first (no web tech: React/Electron/Next.js). Visual-first: graph editor is the center, no code needed for common tasks. PyTorch native: only backend. Unreal Blueprint / Blender Geometry Nodes / LabVIEW as inspiration. Long-term goal: complete ML environment without leaving the app.
